sections.content:
After clicking (11, 40):
- White diamond (W, 12px) moved to (11, 40) - from (12, 32)
- Orange ring (O, 20px) moved to (13, 32) - from (13, 29)
- Magenta (M, 1px) at (13, 32)
The white diamond is now at (11, 40), continuing toward (9, 49). The path continues through (10, 44).
Let me continue clicking path segments to advance the white diamond to the destination.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White diamond at (11,40), next path segment is (10,44), then destination (9,49)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 44}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After path click:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] < 30 and n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'p', 'O', 'M', 'w']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
